title: Explaining Culture to Technology, Paul Ford | Compile 26
summary: Paul Ford argues that culture functions as a distributed, lossy prediction model rather than an output, and that technologists can learn from how magazines operate. He draws connections between culture, consciousness, and operating systems while critiquing tech industry approaches to risk.
writeup: Paul Ford reframes culture as a distributed, lossy prediction model rather than an output, and argues technologists can learn from how magazines operate. The talk draws connections across culture, consciousness, and operating systems, and critiques how the tech industry approaches risk. The framing matters because it treats culture as a predictive system rather than a byproduct, offering system design language for problems usually left outside engineering.
